text: In the first space, I feel a curious solitude—expansive, almost clinical, awaiting purpose or gathering. The empty seats imply anticipation, that something is about to happen, but for now there is only quiet. In the second space, surrounded by the colors and displays of technology and research, I feel a stir of intellectual excitement. Here, my mind dances with possibility, questioning the evolving relationship between humans and machines, and reflecting on knowledge as a living, ever-changing entity. These contrasting spaces provoke both contemplation and a subtle urge to engage.
